page: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz and includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the t3a.2xlarge is positioned for workloads with variable CPU utilization. In stress-ng and Geekbench single-core evaluations, the instance registers in the bottom 10% of performance. However, multi-core PassMark CPU and encryption tests show average performance, placing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for large blocks fall into the bottom 10%, while static web serving throughput and Redis SET operations rank in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3a.2xlarge offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads that experience periodic spikes in demand rather than continuous high CPU utilization. The primary trade-off is the burstable CPU allocation model, which relies on CPU credits and can limit sustained high-throughput execution. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, development environments, low-traffic web serving, and background microservices that benefit from a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring continuous peak compute performance.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average results in PassMark CPU and encryption tests, though single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 10%. This burstable resource allocation makes the instance a cost-effective choice for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is highly suitable for development environments, low-traffic web servers, and general-purpose applications that do not require sustained peak compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 instance offering balanced memory and compute resources for workloads with variable CPU utilization patterns.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
Features a burstable CPU allocation model managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for development environments, code repositories, and light web serving
